title: Packit/RPM: build with sequoia for f43+ and couple of other changes for distro conditionals and easier testing

comments.body:
@mtrmac PTAL. Do we need anything apart from depending on podman-sequoia and building with `containers_image_sequoia` ?

cc @jnovy

> @lsm5 I see testing-farm tests failing on F43/rawhide now, e.g. https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/8be93fae-957e-41cf-963e-344e0daa8673/ in “signing” because of an unexpected “Missing ke y” error text.
> 
> I think that’s because the CI images use `rust-podman-sequoia` 0.1.0, and the RPMs are now 0.2.0 (specifically [ueno/podman-sequoia@d41fefa](https://github.com/ueno/podman-sequoia/commit/d41fefaa1fa35ee56c6a3aac0105232733460932) replaces the expected “was not found” error message).

So, IIUC, the skopeo tests need to be fixed themselves to account for v0.2.0 podman-sequoia, is that right? Once that's done, the testing-farm jobs should go green. 

Just to ensure we're on the same page RE: testing-farm jobs, the CI images maintained via automation_images will not have any effect on testing-farm jobs. They'll just fetch the composes + repos maintained by the testing-farm team.

> It would be cleanest to update the CI images to 0.2.0, _and_ to include `rust-podman-sequoia` in the F43 image, for consistency — but, short-term, that should be coordinated with [containers/podman#27017 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27017#issuecomment-3267081316) .

Looks like podman-sequoia is [already being included](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/blob/main/cache_images/fedora_packaging.sh#L180) ?

----
BTW, WDYT about getting rid of Cirrus for cases where we need to use podman-sequoia and only use Packit/TMT jobs? That'll help us proceed toward https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2633 . For safety, we can just disable all Cirrus jobs but still retain the config. 

For the immediate term, I'm cool with fixing automation_images in the open PR itself if that's what it takes to get CI going. 

I've got some WIP to enable the other test jobs via Packit/TMT, but I'm gonna be away the rest of this week and will only resume next week.

> Looks like podman-sequoia is [already being included](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/blob/main/cache_images/fedora_packaging.sh#L180) ?

NVM, I see it was only for rawhide.

> So, IIUC, the skopeo tests need to be fixed themselves to account for v0.2.0 podman-sequoia, is that right? Once that's done, the testing-farm jobs should go green.

Yes. I’ll update the tests in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2645 .

> BTW, WDYT about getting rid of Cirrus for cases where we need to use podman-sequoia and only use Packit/TMT jobs? That'll help us proceed toward #2633 .

I don’t have much of an opinion. As noted in #2633, we do want to test _some_ builds with options that are not shipped in Fedora / CentOS, so I’m not sure we can get rid of Cirrus entirely, but for those cases that are ~equivalent, up to you.

Do note that, right now, Packit is only running system tests, not integration tests (and I’m not sure about unit tests). So I don’t think we can cleanly switch _at this moment_.

> For the immediate term, I'm cool with fixing automation_images in the open PR itself if that's what it takes to get CI going.

`automation_images` are (were?) being updated for Podman, I was told not to modify that repo yesterday. So, for now, I’ll update the Skopeo tests to accept both versions.

> BTW, WDYT about getting rid of Cirrus for cases where we need to use podman-sequoia and only use Packit/TMT jobs?

I didn’t collect numbers — purely anecdotally, the `testing-farm` jobs tend to take longer than Cirrus ones. Is there something we can easily do to improve that?

> > BTW, WDYT about getting rid of Cirrus for cases where we need to use podman-sequoia and only use Packit/TMT jobs?
> 
> I didn’t collect numbers — purely anecdotally, the `testing-farm` jobs tend to take longer than Cirrus ones. Is there something we can easily do to improve that?

I suspect it's because of the testing-farm jobs depending on the copr build jobs. We could have testing-farm jobs independent of copr builds, but perhaps for system tests, ostree tests (WIP) we should depend on the rpm. Validate, unit and maybe even integration could run independently, so those should go faster.
